findings.evidence: Mathlib already has `MonoidAlgebra.curryAlgEquiv` with `curryAlgEquiv_single` at `mathlib/Mathlib/Algebra/MonoidAlgebra/Basic.lean:165-178` and `MonoidAlgebra.tensorEquiv` with tensor/single simp lemmas at `mathlib/Mathlib/RingTheory/TensorProduct/MonoidAlgebra.lean:56-85`; the PR duplicates their universal-property construction at lines 67-130.
findings.fix: Define the algebra equivalence as a composition of `MonoidAlgebra.curryAlgEquiv`/`commAlgEquiv` with `MonoidAlgebra.tensorEquiv` (and tensor comm/unit equivalences as needed), then add only the bialgebra compatibility proof on top.
findings.issue: `prodTensorAlgEquiv` hand-rolls the algebra equivalence `R[G × H] ≃ₐ[R] R[G] ⊗[R] R[H]` instead of reusing Mathlib's existing monoid-algebra currying/base-change tensor equivalences.

findings.evidence: TauCeti has `DiagonalizableGroup.pointsMulEquiv : WithConv (MonoidAlgebra R G →ₐ[R] A) ≃* (G →* Aˣ)` at `code/TauCeti/Algebra/AlgebraicGroup/DiagonalizableGroup.lean:182-193`; Mathlib has `MonoidHom.coprod`, `coprod_comp_inl`, `coprod_comp_inr`, and `coprod_unique` at `mathlib/Mathlib/Algebra/Group/Prod.lean:460-477`, giving the product decomposition of `(G × H →* Aˣ)`.
findings.fix: Reuse `DiagonalizableGroup.pointsMulEquiv` on `G × H`, `G`, and `H`, together with the equivalence induced by restriction along `MonoidHom.inl`/`inr` and inverse `MonoidHom.coprod`; if the tensor-product route is kept, prove it agrees with that character-based equivalence instead of exposing an unrelated parallel construction.
findings.issue: `DiagonalizableGroup.prodPointsMulEquiv` is a parallel points API for a result already obtained from the existing character equivalence for diagonalizable groups plus Mathlib's product universal property for `MonoidHom`.

summary: The PR adds useful bialgebra packaging, but parts of the new API reprove existing Mathlib/TauCeti product machinery instead of building on it.
